commit.message:
🎯 Adaptive Update Instructions Based on Deployment Method (#238)

* feat: adaptive update instructions based on deployment method

Resolves the issue where update notification emails always showed Docker Compose
instructions regardless of the actual deployment method used by users.

Update notifications always displayed './scripts/update.sh' (Docker Compose only)
but Qarote supports 4 deployment methods with different update procedures:
- Docker Compose: './scripts/update.sh'
- Dokku: 'git push dokku main'
- Binary: curl + tar + restart commands
- Manual/Source: git pull + npm install + build

- **DeploymentDetector**: Analyzes runtime environment to identify deployment method
- **DeploymentService**: Manages detection persistence and retrieval
- Detects based on environment variables, file system structure, and process info

- **UpdateAvailableEmail**: Now accepts deployment-specific instructions
- **NotificationEmailService**: Fetches and passes appropriate instructions
- Maintains backward compatibility with default Docker Compose instructions

- **Dokku**: 'git push dokku main' with deployment explanation
- **Docker Compose**: './scripts/update.sh' (existing behavior)
- **Binary**: Multi-step curl/tar/restart commands
- **Manual**: git pull + dependency installation + build steps

- Runs on application startup and stores result in SystemState table
- Cached for performance, re-detected if not found
- Graceful fallback to Docker Compose method if detection fails

- apps/api/src/services/deployment/deployment-detector.ts
- apps/api/src/services/deployment/deployment.service.ts
- DEPLOYMENT_DETECTION_SOLUTION.md

- apps/api/src/services/email/templates/update-available-email.tsx
- apps/api/src/services/email/notification-email.service.tsx
- apps/api/src/server.ts (added initialization call)

- ✅ Correct update instructions for each deployment method
- ✅ Improved user experience and update adoption
- ✅ Reduced support burden from incorrect instructions
- ✅ Maintains backward compatibility

- Deployment detection logic handles edge cases
- Email template gracefully falls back to default instructions
- No breaking changes to existing functionality

* refactor: consolidate binary and manual deployment methods

Based on feedback that binary and manual are now unified in the official
deployment documentation, this commit:

- Removes 'manual' as a separate deployment method
- Updates DeploymentMethod type to only include: dokku, docker_compose, binary
- Updates binary detection to handle both binary and manual/source deployments
- Updates binary update instructions to include platform auto-detection
- Adds development rules documentation for branch management

Binary method now covers both standalone binary deployments and
manual/source deployments, as they use similar update procedures.

* fix: address review findings from pr #238

- Prevent Docker Compose false positives by requiring Docker runtime
  evidence before checking compose files
- Use path-segment matching instead of substring for binary detection
- Validate stored deployment method against known values
- Quote shell variable and enable pipefail in dependabot script
- Fix markdown formatting and extract inline email styles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve self-hosted plan to license tier instead of always free

The plan system and license system were independent — activating a
license enabled premium features but getCurrentPlan always returned
FREE because it only checked the Stripe Subscription table. Now falls
back to the license JWT tier in self-hosted mode.

Also scopes rate limiter to /trpc/* routes only (was blocking static
assets with 429s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: invalidate api license cache in e2e test hooks

Call selfhostedLicense.deactivate via API before db.clearSystemSetting
to ensure the 60s in-memory license cache is invalidated between tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: assert plans tab visibility instead of conditional click

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update email template prop from deploymentMethod to updateInstructions,
update release notifier example, and correct fallback from
docker_compose to binary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make deployment method persistence best-effort

Wrap storeDeploymentMethod in its own try/catch so a persistence
failure doesn't discard a successfully detected method.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove phantom qarote.config.json check from binary detection

This file doesn't exist in any deployment method, so the check was
dead code that could never match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove stale manual/source deployment references

Binary is now the manual deployment method; there is no separate
manual deployment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: include git pull step in dokku update instructions

The previous command only showed the deploy step; users need to pull
the latest Qarote source before pushing to their Dokku remote.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: require update instructions in email template

Remove optional marker and default fallback so callers must always
provide deployment-specific update instructions.

No longer needed after dependabot consolidation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Blocks puzzle game

Drag-and-drop (tap-to-select + tap-to-place) block puzzle where players
fit 6 coloured pieces onto an irregular 26-cell board. Features:
- 5×6 board with unique shape (extra cell protrudes in row 3)
- 6 pieces: blue 2×3, yellow L-pentomino, green/purple 2×2, cyan bar, red S
- Smart snap: tries all piece-cell alignments for the tapped position
- Easy mode: hint cells pulse to show valid placements
- Hard mode: 5-minute countdown via GameShell
- Elapsed timer + pieces-placed progress counter
- Fully accessible (keyboard navigation, aria-labels)

commit.message:
Fix BCT nan risk, softmax recomputation, layer_selection validation, double .item()

- BehavioralConsistencyLoss: replace inline JSD with _jsd() helper to avoid
  nan from unclamped .log() on zero probabilities
- BehavioralConsistencyLoss: compute clean_probs/adv_probs once and reuse
  for both loss computation and monitoring metrics
- ActivationConsistencyLoss: raise ValueError for unrecognised layer_selection
  strings instead of silently falling back to "all"
- Trainer: cache loss_dict["loss"].item() to avoid two GPU-CPU syncs per step

commit.message:
feat: キャリアコーチングスキル初回リリース

ICF標準と日本のキャリア市場に特化したコーチングスキル。
GROWモデル・バランスホイール・Will/Can/Must等を収録。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify CMake build: target-scoped properties, cleanup (#356)

* Simplify CMake build: GLOB_RECURSE, target-scoped properties, cleanup

- Replace 28 subdirectory CMakeLists.txt with file(GLOB_RECURSE CONFIGURE_DEPENDS)
- Replace global include_directories/add_compile_definitions with target-scoped equivalents
- Replace find_library for numa with pkg-config (PkgConfig::NUMA)
- Move CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS to CMake presets
- Inline cuCascade setup directly in CMakeLists.txt, remove third_party/
- Remove unused: ENABLE_CUDF define, -rdc=true flag, -std=c++20 flag,
  SKIP_SUBSTRAIT_C_TESTS, CUDA --expt-* flags (default since CUDA 12.2)
- Remove bundled DuckDB extensions (json, tpcds, tpch, parquet, icu) from
  extension_config.cmake
- Remove orphaned sources: memmove.cu, downgrade_task_creator.{cpp,hpp}
- Remove commented-out dead code (substrait refs, old link lines)
- Consolidate set_target_properties and target_link_libraries per target
- Exclude stale disabled tests (test_modified_pipeline, test_task_creator)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Format

Restore parquet extension

* Fix segfault: restore previous cudf device resource on destruction

sirius_memory_reservation_manager's destructor called
cudf::reset_current_device_resource_ref() which left a dangling
resource_ref after the underlying memory space was destroyed. This
caused a NULL function pointer call in RMM when subsequent code
(e.g. cudf's parquet reader) allocated device memory through the
global cudf device resource.

Save the previous resource in the constructor and restore it in the
destructor instead of resetting to a default that references the
already-destroyed allocator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update pixi.lock

* Deduplicate CMake config with foreach, generator expressions, cleanup

- Replace repeated sirius_extension/sirius_loadable_extension blocks with
  a single foreach loop for shared properties, includes, definitions, and
  link libraries
- Use $<BUILD_INTERFACE:...> for include directories and path-based
  compile definitions, following cucascade's patterns
- Remove redundant per-target CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION (already set
  globally via CMAKE_CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION)
- Consolidate install rules into one call with comment explaining the
  DuckDB export set requirement; remove unused stream_check install
- Remove setup_sirius.sh (superseded by pixi)
- Inline TEST_NAME variable; add FATAL_ERROR to cmake_minimum_required

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use mold linker for faster link times

Add mold to pixi dependencies and set CMAKE_LINKER_TYPE=MOLD in the
base CMake preset so all build configurations use it by default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format

* Set CUDA_RESOLVE_DEVICE_SYMBOLS globally, remove per-target property

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove explicit LINKER_LANGUAGE CUDA, CMake infers it from .cu sources

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use CONFIG mode for libconfig++ and absl find_package calls

Ensures deterministic package resolution from the pixi environment
instead of falling back to system Find-modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Normalize include paths to use only src/include

Remove src/include/operator and src/include/cudf from the CMake include
directories. Instead, qualify every include of a header living under
those subdirectories with its operator/ or cudf/ prefix so that a single
-Isrc/include entry suffices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove 430 unused #include directives across 119 files

Ran clang-include-cleaner with --edit --disable-insert (removals only,
no insertions) on all .cpp files under src/ and test/. Restored 8 files
where the tool produced false positives due to transitive dependencies,
implicit conversions, or incomplete type requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format

* Add precompiled headers to reduce build times

Precompile frequently-included headers for extension targets (STL,
spdlog/logging, DuckDB planner) and the Catch2 header for tests.
The loadable extension reuses the static extension's PCH to avoid
compiling it twice. CUDA sources are excluded since nvcc doesn't
support CMake's PCH mechanism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format

* Format

* Fix debug build

* Fix mixed right semi/anti join inequality operator not flipped on side swap

When swap_sides=true (used for RIGHT_SEMI and RIGHT_ANTI mixed joins),
the table references for left/right expressions were correctly swapped,
but the inequality comparison operators were not flipped. This meant
e.g. "L.a > R.b" became "R.a > L.b" instead of the correct "R.a < L.b",
producing wrong join results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Ensure CMake presets symlink exists before build targets

The Makefile creates a duckdb/CMakePresets.json symlink pointing to
cmake/CMakePresets.json as a prerequisite of all build targets. This
ensures cmake --preset works even if the pixi activation script hasn't
run (e.g. when invoking make directly outside pixi).

Use CMakePresets.json (not CMakeUserPresets.json) because CMake 4.x
requires a CMakePresets.json to exist alongside CMakeUserPresets.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert translator operator flip, update DuckDB to 1.4.4

The mixed right semi/anti join test failure is caused by a DuckDB bug
(duckdb/duckdb#20410) fixed in v1.4.4 (commit fc87d53e32), not by the
expression translator. Revert the incorrect operator flip and update
the DuckDB submodule from d1dc88f950 (v1.4.3) to 6ddac802 (v1.4.4)
to match the dev branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix include path for cudf_utils.hpp after dev merge

Code from dev used the old-style #include <cudf_utils.hpp>, but
cmake-cleanup normalized includes to use the cudf/ subdirectory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format

* Update submodule to match dev

* Remove unused #include directives flagged by clang-include-cleaner

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move language standard settings from global scope to target level

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace GLOB_RECURSE with explicit file lists in CMakeLists.txt

Avoid file(GLOB_RECURSE CONFIGURE_DEPENDS) for source files due to
portability, build performance, and accidental inclusion risks. List all
source files explicitly for EXTENSION_SOURCES, CUDA_SOURCES, and
TEST_SOURCES.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

old SVG underline with a CSS background-size keyframe animation.

feat: email field always visible + Pakistan phone/email validation on checkout

- Email field now shown for all users (authenticated + guest), pre-filled
  from account but editable; labelled as "used for order confirmation"
- Phone: onKeyDown blocks all non-digit/non-formatting keys (letters, symbols);
  onPaste blocks invalid paste; validates 03XXXXXXXXX / +923XXXXXXXXX / 0092...
- Email: blocks spaces on keypress; validates format, consecutive dots,
  missing domain TLD, invalid characters

  (once touched); border turns red on error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add missing role to user messages so ADK doesn't drop them (#239)

The ADK's getContents() filters out events without content.role set,
causing user messages to be silently dropped and the agent to respond
with a generic welcome. Also fix session replay to use createEvent()
for proper event fields, and add mute/pause/stop to Sonos keywords.

commit.message:
Replace Notion API with Gemini URL Context for Rork changelog

Notion APIでは他者のページを認証の関係で取得できないため、
Gemini APIのURLContext toolを使いNotionページを直接読ませる方式に変更。
変更検知は要約テキストのSHA256ハッシュで行う。

feat: Gate 4 complete — JSON-RPC server, devnet launcher, integration test

Adds the eth_ namespace JSON-RPC server (eth_chainId, eth_blockNumber,
eth_getBalance, eth_getBlockByNumber, eth_getTransactionReceipt,
eth_getTransactionCount, eth_gasPrice), a full devnet binary integrating
P2P + consensus + execution + RPC, and an end-to-end integration test
that produces 5 blocks through the consensus pipeline and verifies
state via RPC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The md:w-1/2 divs had no width on mobile, causing the gallery
(which uses w-full + aspect ratio) to collapse to zero width

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add peek-scroll gallery and all hiking photos

Rework JourneyStepGallery to show adjacent photos peeking from the
sides (24px peek + 8px gap) so users know they can scroll. Move dot
indicators below the image. Add all 7 mountain photos to the
Discovering Hiking timeline step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: rework gallery to card stack with slanted side cards

Center image sits on top (z-10), prev/next cards peek from behind
with 4deg rotation, 92% scale, and a dark overlay. Clicking a side
card or using arrows/dots animates the transition (500ms ease-out).
No scroll-based logic — pure state-driven with CSS transitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add touch swipe and fix card stack overflow

Add touch swipe support (50px threshold) on the center card for
mobile navigation. Reduce side card size (80% width, 3deg rotation,
0.9 scale) and add px-4 padding to prevent cards from protruding
outside the container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fluid drag-to-swipe for card stack gallery

Center card follows finger in real-time during drag. Side cards
interpolate rotation/scale/overlay toward center as you drag toward
them. Releases use velocity + distance threshold to decide snap vs
bounce-back. Easing uses cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for a
natural spring-like settle. Rubber-band effect at edges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

defaults, add /about to sitemap and breadcrumb routes, and add
reusable PageShareButtons component to the About hero section.

Fix XIRR calculator portfolio save - extract correct values

Added proper case for XIRR calculator in SaveToPortfolioButton to extract:
- totalInvestment from result.totalInvested or property.totalPrice
- ROI from result.rate (converted from decimal to percentage)
- avgCashFlow calculated from netProfit / holdPeriodMonths
- breakEvenMonths from holdPeriodMonths

Also added XIRR to scorable calculators for investment scoring.

feat: add subagent support with streaming UI and WebSocket chat

- Add SubagentConfigSchema to shared package (name, description, prompt, tools, model, maxTurns)
- Add taskToolEnabled toggle for dynamic subagent creation via Task tool
- Backend ConfigService: buildAgentsConfig() converts SubagentConfig[] to SDK AgentDefinition
- Backend streaming-session: propagate parent_tool_use_id for subagent event routing
- Frontend AgentConfigsPage: inline subagent CRUD with validation and tool selection
- Frontend ToolCallDisplay: rich SubagentToolDisplay showing streaming text, thinking, nested tool calls
- Frontend useChat: route events with parentToolUseId to parent ToolCall's sub-state
- WebSocket-based streaming chat replacing POST+SSE (chat-ws, streaming-session, AsyncQueue)
- Session sidebar, slash commands, todo panel, topic auto-generation
- Multi-modal support (images, PDF attachments)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use ResizeObserver to ensure autoscroll reaches true bottom

The synchronous autoScroll() call can miss the actual bottom when
scrollHeight hasn't updated after layout reflow. A ResizeObserver on
chatInner fires post-reflow and pins scroll to the correct position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Seek scrolls to target message instead of forcing to bottom

seekTo now scrolls the last rendered message into view at the top of
the viewport and disables auto-scroll, letting the user review the
content at that point in the timeline. Auto-scroll re-enables when
the user scrolls back to the bottom.
